title: The World And The Quietist
content:
آ آ آ آ Why, when the Worldï؟½s great mind
آ آ آ آ Hath finally inclinï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ Why, you say, Critias, be debating still?
آ آ آ آ Why, with these mournful rhymes
آ آ آ آ Learnï؟½d in more languid climes,
آ آ آ آ Blame our activity,
آ آ آ آ Who, with such passionate will,
آ آ آ آ Are, what we mean to be?

آ آ آ آ Critias, long since, I know,
آ آ آ آ (For Fate decreed it so,)
آ آ آ آ Long since the World hath set its heart to live.
آ آ آ آ Long since with credulous zeal
آ آ آ آ It turns Lifeï؟½s mighty wheel;
آ آ آ آ Still doth for labourers send,
آ آ آ آ Who still their labour give;
آ آ آ آ And still expects an end.

آ آ آ آ Yet, as the wheel flies round,
آ آ آ آ With no ungrateful sound
آ آ آ آ Do adverse voices fall on the Worldï؟½s ear.
آ آ آ آ Deafenï؟½d by his own stir
آ آ آ آ The rugged Labourer
آ آ آ آ Caught not till then a sense
آ آ آ آ So glowing and so near
آ آ آ آ Of his omnipotence.

آ آ آ آ So, when the feast grew loud
آ آ آ آ In Susaï؟½s palace proud,
آ آ آ آ A white-robï؟½d slave stole to the Monarchï؟½s side.
آ آ آ آ He spoke: the Monarch heard:
آ آ آ آ Felt the slow-rolling word
آ آ آ آ Swell his attentive soul.
آ آ آ آ Breathï؟½d deeply as it died,
آ آ آ آ And drainï؟½d his mighty bowl.
meaning:
"The World And The Quietist" is a quintessential example of Matthew Arnold's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
